stages.title: The capture
stages.blurb: A phone photo on a table — angled, unevenly lit, curling at the edges.

stages.title: Even the lighting
stages.blurb: We estimate the illumination field and divide it out, so paper reads uniformly bright regardless of shadow or glare.

stages.title: Find the paper
stages.blurb: A threshold on the normalized image isolates the sheet as one clean shape — robust to wood grain and shadow.

stages.title: The page mask
stages.blurb: Bridges are severed, holes filled, and boundary notches repaired to leave a single crisp page silhouette.

stages.title: Map the edges
stages.blurb: The four page edges drive a Coons patch — a UV grid that knows exactly how the paper is warped in space.

stages.title: Lift it flat
stages.blurb: The patch un-warps perspective and curl, reprojecting the page onto a true rectangle.

stages.title: Clean the ink
stages.blurb: Uneven lighting is removed and the ink is rendered as fine, anti-aliased soft grayscale.

stages.title: Straighten staves
stages.blurb: Using the staff lines themselves, residual waviness and skew are ironed flat and the margins squared.
